2020-03-26.title: Man Pleads Guilty to New Zealand Mosque Massacre
2020-03-26.abstract: An Australian white supremacist charged with killing 51 worshipers at two mosques in New Zealand last year changed his plea to guilty in a surprise move.

2020-03-26.title: When Your Restaurant’s Star Dish Is Blamed for Spreading Coronavirus
2020-03-26.abstract: As restaurants around the world close or retool to enforce social distancing, Hong Kong’s hot pot eateries offer a cautionary tale and some good advice.

2020-03-26.title: U.S. Now Leads the World in Confirmed Cases
2020-03-26.abstract: Unemployment data set a bleak record and hospitals struggled with an influx of sick patients and lack of equipment. But the White House coronavirus response coordinator dismissed talk of shortages.

2020-03-26.title: Fresh From Iran’s Coronavirus Zone, Now Moving Across Afghanistan
2020-03-26.abstract: Thousands of Afghans returning each day bring a risk of widespread infections to the border city of Herat and beyond in a country with little capacity to test for or treat Covid-19.

2020-03-26.title: ‘God Will Protect Us’: Coronavirus Spreads Through an Already Struggling Pakistan
2020-03-26.abstract: While Pakistan’s government and military are at odds over how to respond, doctors are protesting to get equipment and more testing, and clerics are refusing to limit mosque gatherings.

2020-03-27.title: For France, Coronavirus Tests a Vaunted Health Care System
2020-03-27.abstract: The country goes into the battle with the contagion better prepared than most. Even so, a doubling of cases every four days has the structure creaking and straining.

2020-03-27.title: Trump Signs $2 Trillion Bill as U.S. Virus Cases Pass 100,000
2020-03-27.abstract: President Trump, who had questioned the need for additional ventilators, pushed industry to make more. A new survey of mayors found dire shortages of urgently needed supplies.

2020-03-27.title: What if Restaurants and Pubs Disappear in Australia?
2020-03-27.abstract: The relaxed country is known for its rich restaurant and bar culture. What will happen if its hospitality industry crumbles?

2020-03-27.title: The Virus Is Coming to Myanmar, but the Pain Has Already Begun
2020-03-27.abstract: Economic fallout from the pandemic has upended lives in the impoverished country, where the first coronavirus cases are only now being reported.

2020-03-27.title: ‘We Take the Dead From Morning Till Night’
2020-03-27.abstract: No country has been hit harder by the coronavirus than Italy, and no province has suffered as many losses as Bergamo. Photos and voices from there evoke a portrait of despair.

2020-03-28.title: U.S. Cuts Health Care Aid to Yemen Despite Worries About Coronavirus
2020-03-28.abstract: The Trump administration cited interference by Houthi rebels. But humanitarian groups and some Democrats asked for a delay because of the pandemic.

2020-03-28.title: Trump Says a Quarantine ‘Will Not Be Necessary’
2020-03-28.abstract: At least 17 states reported tallies of at least 1,000 infections. Illinois reported the first known U.S. death of an infant with the virus.

2020-03-28.title: In the Coronavirus Fight in Scandinavia, Sweden Stands Apart
2020-03-28.abstract: The country has drawn global attention with an unorthodox approach while its neighbors have imposed extensive restrictions.

2020-03-28.title: Frightened by Coronavirus, Many of U.K.’s Poles Are Heading Home
2020-03-28.abstract: They say the government’s slow response and a creaking health service contrast poorly with Poland’s health system and aggressive actions. The epidemic also put many of them out of work.

2020-03-28.title: As Pandemic Rages, Sri Lanka’s President Pardons a War Criminal
2020-03-28.abstract: Rights groups accused President Gotabaya Rajapaksa of taking advantage of the global chaos to free a soldier convicted of killing civilians.

2020-03-29.title: North Korea Launches Two Short-Range Ballistic Missiles
2020-03-29.abstract: The country has carried out a handful of similar tests this month. The missiles fell into waters between the North and Japan.

2020-03-29.title: Trump Extends Social Distancing Guidelines to April 30 as U.S. Cases Top 140,000
2020-03-29.abstract: The global count has passed 700,000, an official warns Britain that some kind of lockdown may last for months and Joe Biden urges mail-in elections.

2020-03-29.title: India’s Coronavirus Lockdown Leaves Vast Numbers Stranded and Hungry
2020-03-29.abstract: The sudden shutdown of businesses has upended the lives of millions of migrant laborers in Indian cities. More than a dozen migrants have died, and anger is rising.

2020-03-29.title: Egypt’s Female Lion Tamers Show the Men How to Do It
2020-03-29.abstract: The broader struggle for women’s equality may be lagging in Egypt, but six women dominate the field of lion taming in the country.

2020-03-29.title: Rich Europeans Flee Virus for 2nd Homes, Spreading Fear and Fury
2020-03-29.abstract: In France and the rest of Europe, the affluent decamp cities to spend their confinement in vacation homes, widening class divides.

2020-03-30.title: As Governors Plead for Tests, Trump Promises Ventilators to Europe
2020-03-30.abstract: Maryland, Virginia and Arizona issued statewide stay-at-home orders, and the F.D.A. granted emergency approval for the use of two malaria drugs to treat some coronavirus patients.

2020-03-30.title: Bogotá’s Cacophony Is Hushed by Virus as Congested Streets Empty Out
2020-03-30.abstract: In normal times, Colombia’s crowded capital reverberates with never-ending noise. But under quarantine, the city was overtaken by an extraordinary sound: silence.

2020-03-30.title: Xinjiang Returns to Work, but Coronavirus Worries Linger in China
2020-03-30.abstract: The Chinese region, where hundreds of thousands of Muslims are held in indoctrination camps, has been an information void. Uighurs fear a lockdown in the region drove many to hunger.

2020-03-30.title: For Autocrats, and Others, Coronavirus Is a Chance to Grab Even More Power
2020-03-30.abstract: Leaders around the world have passed emergency decrees and legislation expanding their reach during the pandemic. Will they ever relinquish them?

2020-03-30.title: North Korea Says Pompeo Made It Lose All Interest in Dialogue With U.S.
2020-03-30.abstract: Accusing the secretary of state of slander, the North says he is going against President Trump’s will.

2020-03-31.title: White House Projects Grim Toll From Coronavirus
2020-03-31.abstract: The scientists leading the administration’s fight estimated the virus could kill between 100,000 and 240,000 Americans. New data suggests many as 25 percent of infected people may not show symptoms.

2020-03-31.title: Quarreling in Quarantine and Bridging a Generational Divide
2020-03-31.abstract: A lockdown brings together a Chinese student wary of the government and his parents, devoted to it.

2020-03-31.title: China Chases Indonesia’s Fishing Fleets, Staking Claim to Sea’s Riches
2020-03-31.abstract: The Indonesian government appears to have backed away from confronting China, its largest trading partner. “Our fishermen feel scared,” one official said.

2020-03-31.title: North Korea Claims No Coronavirus Cases. Can It Be Trusted?
2020-03-31.abstract: Decades of isolation and international sanctions have ravaged North Korea’s public health system, raising fears about its ability to confront an outbreak.

2020-03-31.title: U.S. Counts on Global Crises to Press Again for Power Shift in Venezuela
2020-03-31.abstract: Yet there is no indication Nicolás Maduro will step down, despite little assistance to confront the coronavirus and losing an economic lifeline during an oil spat between Russia and Saudi Arabia.
